---
title: "Overview of USRDS analysis"
format: html
---

## United States Renal Data System (USRDS)

The [**United States Renal Data System (USRDS)**](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/about-niddk/strategic-plans-reports/usrds) is a major U.S. resource for kidney disease research, containing linked data used to study chronic kidney disease, kidney failure, dialysis, transplantation, and related outcomes. Because the database is large, multi-part, and changes over time, using it can be analytically complex.

This project examines **how researchers use the USRDS in published studies**. Through structured review of full-text PDFs, we identify what database components were used, what kinds of analyses were performed, and what programming languages or methods were reported.

## Methods

We manually reviewed a sample of papers using USRDS data and recorded study characteristics, reported programming languages, and the files or data elements used. We then asked multiple large language models to extract the same information from the papers and compared model outputs with human review using descriptive summaries and agreement statistics.

## Results

Manual review showed substantial variation in how clearly papers reported key details relevant to reproducibility, including programming language and data file use. Across the LLM analyses, model performance varied by task, with better agreement for some broad study-level classifications than for more specific extraction tasks.

See the detailed results pages linked via the left sidebar for full details.

## Reproducibility

All analyses presented on this website are fully reproducible.  

The complete source code used to perform the full analysis and generate the tables and figures is publicly available at this [GitHub repo](https://github.com/VagishHemmige/USRDS-PDF-analysis-project).


## Abstracts/manuscripts

This analysis has yet to be published to date.

Abstracts have been presented or will be presented at:

- World Transplant Congress 2025
- American Transplant Congress 2026
